name: Tadalafil Mood, Well-Being, And Quality Of Life Baseline Search

search_queries.query: tadalafil mood depression well-being quality of life randomized trial PubMed
tadalafil self-esteem relationship questionnaire erectile dysfunction randomized placebo PubMed
tadalafil lower urinary tract symptoms quality of life IPSS QoL randomized trial
tadalafil mental well-being quality of life cerebral small vessel disease trial

inclusion_criteria: Human tadalafil or closely relevant PDE5-inhibitor evidence with mood, depressive-symptom, anxiety, self-esteem, relationship, subjective well-being, treatment satisfaction, vitality, sleep-adjacent, or quality-of-life endpoints.
Prefer tadalafil-specific randomized, controlled, or prospectively measured evidence.
Include ED/BPH/CSVD evidence only when endpoint directness and population limits remain explicit.
Include existing project sources when they directly report mental well-being or quality-of-life endpoints.
exclusion_criteria: Animal, in vitro, or mechanism-only evidence without human mood, well-being, or quality-of-life outcomes.
Sources measuring only erectile function, urinary flow, or cardiovascular biomarkers without patient-reported mood/well-being/QOL endpoints.
Non-tadalafil PDE5 evidence unless it materially changes the class-transfer boundary.
Reddit, lay commentary, marketing pages, or unsupported anecdotal reports.
dedupe_method: PMID/DOI/title dedupe; when overlapping tadalafil ED psychosocial studies existed, representative randomized tadalafil-specific sources were staged rather than every secondary analysis.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 70 across targeted PubMed and web searches

screening_decisions.title: Improvements in confidence, sexual relationship and satisfaction measures: results of a randomized trial of tadalafil 5 mg taken once daily.
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific placebo-controlled ED trial with SEAR, self-esteem, relationship, confidence, and satisfaction endpoints.

screening_decisions.title: Effects of once-daily tadalafil on treatment satisfaction, psychosocial outcomes, spontaneous erections, and measures of endothelial function in men with erectile dysfunction but naive to phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitors.
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific placebo-controlled ED trial with SEAR total score, treatment satisfaction, and adverse-event context.

screening_decisions.title: An open-label, single-arm pilot study to evaluate the efficacy of daily low dose tadalafil on depression in patients with erectile dysfunction.
screening_decisions.reason: Direct tadalafil depression-symptom endpoint, but low confidence because n=10 and no control group.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil once daily in the treatment of lower urinary tract symptoms suggestive of benign prostatic hyperplasia in men without erectile dysfunction.
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific randomized-trial pooled analysis with non-ED subgroup and IPSS-QoL/BPH Impact Index outcomes, useful to separate non-sexual symptom-QOL from broad well-being.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil Treatment in Patients With Cerebral Small Vessel Disease: The ETLAS-2 Randomized Clinical Trial.
screening_decisions.reason: Existing project source directly reports no mental well-being difference and higher adverse-event frequency in a placebo-controlled tadalafil trial outside ED/BPH.

screening_decisions.title: Effects of tadalafil treatment after bilateral nerve-sparing radical prostatectomy: quality of life, psychosocial outcomes, and treatment satisfaction results from a randomized, placebo-controlled phase IV study.
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific QoL/psychosocial RCT in a highly specific post-prostatectomy population; not staged because the first baseline already includes stronger ED, BPH-without-ED, depression-pilot, and CSVD boundary sources.

screening_decisions.title: Psychosocial outcomes after initial treatment of erectile dysfunction with tadalafil once daily, tadalafil on demand or sildenafil citrate on demand.
screening_decisions.reason: Useful active-comparator ED psychosocial source, but not staged in this tadalafil baseline because placebo-controlled tadalafil ED sources were more direct for causal inference.

screening_decisions.title: A systematic review of the psychosocial outcomes associated with erectile dysfunction.
screening_decisions.reason: Background on ED psychosocial burden rather than tadalafil/PDE5 treatment effect evidence.

screening_decisions.title: Strategies for managing sexual dysfunction induced by antidepressant medication.
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to antidepressant-induced sexual dysfunction management but not a tadalafil mood/well-being effect source for this baseline.

screening_summary: The targeted search found no direct tadalafil trial of mood, well-being, or broad quality of life in mostly healthy men age 30 to 50. Included sources were representative tadalafil-specific human evidence in ED, BPH/LUTS without ED, ED with depressive symptoms, and CSVD mental well-being, each staged with population and endpoint directness limits.
limitations: Targeted baseline search rather than a full systematic review.
Full-text extraction was not completed for most abstract-indexed sources.
ED and BPH quality-of-life outcomes may be mediated by symptom relief rather than direct mood or well-being effects.
New non-tadalafil PDE5 evidence was not staged because the project-level PDE5 transfer framework already requires adjacent evidence to remain labeled.
